markdown.markdown:
ECN 100B – Intermediate Microeconomics

Spring 2025

Prof.  Muehlegger 	

## Announcements

Sections begin this week.

Office hours start this week.  Please see the discussion item on Canvas for times / locations.

Problem Set 1 is posted.  It is due next Tuesday.

The first weekly quiz will happen tomorrow.  

Five multiple choice questions, 10 minutes.

Anytime between 12am – 11:59pm. 

It is open-book, open-note, but not “open-friend” nor “open-AI”

ECN 100B (Muehlegger)

Class 2: Monopoly

2

## Class Outline

What is a monopoly?

Monopoly profit maximization

Welfare  under Monopoly

ECN 100B (Muehlegger)

Class 2: Monopoly

3

## Recap from last class

The perfectly competitive market maximizes welfare. 

There is no deadweight loss and in the long-run, firms produce at the minimum of their average cost curves.

But, there are three common sources of  market failure , situations in which an unregulated market may not maximize welfare.

Firms are not price-takers (e.g., monopoly)

Actions create benefits or impose costs on others (e.g., air pollution)

Some parties have information that others don’t (e.g., used car reliability) 

We begin to explore the first of these today.

ECN 100B (Muehlegger)

Class 2: Monopoly

4

## I. What is a monopoly?

## What is a monopoly?	

A  monopoly  is a sole supplier of a good that has not close substitute.

There many historical (and contemporary) examples of monopolies.

Historical: Hudson Bay Company, British East India Company, US Steel, Aluminum Company of America (ALCOA)

Modern: Many utilities (e.g., PGE), firms with patents (e.g., Pfizer)

ECN 100B (Muehlegger)

Class 2: Monopoly

6

## What are the assumptions of monopoly?

There is a single firm.

The firm sets a single price for all customers.  

Barriers to entry exist

These assumptions lead monopolies to behave differently than perfectly competitive markets.		

A monopoly is  not a price-taker .  The production decision of a monopoly affects the market price.  

If barriers to entry exist, a monopoly can earn economic profits in the long-run.

ECN 100B (Muehlegger)

Class 2: Monopoly

7

## Are monopolies good or bad from a welfare perspective?

Good

Bad

It depends

I don’t know

1:40 – 3:00 (100B – B):  https://join.iclicker.com/MZWB  

ECN 100B (Muehlegger)

Class 2: Monopoly

8

## Marginal revenue for monopoly

An higher production affect monopoly revenue in two ways.

Revenues are higher because the firm sells more units. (Region A)

But, the firm has to cut the price to sell more, so it loses revenue on units that would have sold at the higher price (Region B)

## III. Monopoly and welfare (Perloff 11.2)

## Will consumer and producer surplus be different under a monopoly than perfect competition ?

No.  They will be the same.

Yes.  CS will be higher and PS will be lower.

Yes. CS will be lower and PS will be higher.

Yes. Both CS and PS will be lower.

I don’t know.

ECN 100B (Muehlegger)

Class 2: Monopoly

32

## Will welfare be different under a monopoly than perfect competition?

Yes.  It will be higher.

No. It will be the same.

Yes. It will be lower.

It depends.

I don’t know.

DWL

## Monopoly and Welfare

The monopoly has two effects on welfare.

It has created a DWL triangle ( in gray)  since demand is greater than MC for Q between 200 and 300.

It has captured surplus from the consumers (A)  with higher valuations.  

ECN 100B (Muehlegger)

Class 2: Monopoly

37

P

Q

Monopoly

12

0

6

4

DWL

A

## Major takeaways

Monopolies arise for a variety of reasons, some of which are good from a societal perspective and some of which are bad.  

Like PC firms, monopolies also set MR = MC to maximize profits. 

But, monopolies are not price-takers.  They face a downward sloping demand curve and a downward sloping MR curve.  

Consequently, a profit maximizing monopoly will cut output below the competitive level and raise price.

From a welfare perspective, cutting output below the competitive level creates a DWL triangle.  But, it also transfers surplus from consumers to producers.  

ECN 100B (Muehlegger)

Class 2: Monopoly

38

The first weekly quiz is tomorrow.  You can do it from 12am – 11:59pm, but if you want the full 10 minutes, you need to start it by 11:49pm… 

- The first problem set is posted.  You can find it on Canvas under Files   Problem Sets.  It is due next Tuesday at the start of class.  Please submit it on Canvas.  

- The textbook is available under Bookshelf on Canvas.  Next class, we will  cover Perloff  11.3 – 11.5.

ECN 100B (Muehlegger)

Class 2: Monopoly

39
